readme.title: IGRP Framework — Claude Desktop Coworker
readme.purpose: This folder is designed to be loaded into a Claude Desktop Project as project knowledge. It gives Claude Desktop (claude.ai) the full context of the igrp-framework-nextjs monorepo so it can act as a knowledgeable coworker when you paste in code, ask questions, or draft changes outside your IDE.

setup.step: Open claude.ai and create a new Project.
In the project's Project knowledge, upload every file in this folder as a separate document.
Copy the contents of INSTRUCTIONS.xml into the project's Custom instructions (the system-prompt slot) so Claude Desktop enters every conversation already in the coworker persona.
Start a new chat in that project and describe what you're working on. Claude will pull the right per-project file automatically.

file.name: INSTRUCTIONS.xml
file.role: Main persona + routing rules. Use as custom instructions.

file.name: 00-monorepo-overview.xml
file.role: Workspace layout, commands, dependency order, Tailwind, conventions.

file.name: 03-framework-next-auth.xml
file.role: Auth.js v5 + OAuth2/OIDC + JWT + edge middleware + tsup.

file.name: 04-framework-next-types.xml
file.role: Advanced TypeScript + tsc -b + stable public type APIs.

file.name: 05-framework-next-ui.xml
file.role: Client chrome, providers, React Compiler, next-themes.

file.name: 06-framework-next.xml
file.role: Next 15 server entry — RSC, Server Actions, caching, API client.

file.name: 07-template-demo.xml
file.role: Canonical Next 15 template — IGRPForm/Zod, Biome, preview mode.

tips.tip: The INSTRUCTIONS.xml routing table points Claude at the right per-project file based on the path you mention. Paste file paths into your prompts (e.g., packages/framework/next-ui/src/...) and Claude will pick up the right persona automatically.
Claude Desktop can't run commands. When you ask for a build or test, it will tell you which command to run locally and how to read the result.

For filesystem-aware work (running builds, reading the entire tree, editing across files), use Claude Code in your IDE — the nested CLAUDE.md files in each project folder load the same expertise there automatically.
Keep this folder committed — onboarding a new teammate to the Project just means re-uploading these files.
